input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule S.4.6.2 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: s.4.6.2
            TITLE: vehicles must be presented for design judging in finished condition, fully assembled, complete

            CONTENT:
            rule S.4.6.2: Vehicles must be presented for Design judging in finished condition, fully assembled, complete
S.4.6.2 Vehicles must be presented for Design judging in finished condition, fully assembled, complete 
and ready to run.  
a. The judges will not evaluate any vehicle that is presented at the Design event in what 
they consider to be an unfinished state.   
b. Point penalties may be assessed for vehicles with obvious preparation issues

final_answer: Rule S.4.6.2: Vehicles must be presented for Design judging in finished condition, fully assembled, complete and ready to run. a. The judges will not evaluate any vehicle that is presented at the Design event in what they consider to be an unfinished state. b. Point penalties may be assessed for vehicles with obvious preparation issues.
